channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump makes his first moves on education, but no McMahon hearing in sight
item.description: President Trump made his first major moves on education Wednesday, signing a series of executive orders that come even before his nominee to lead the Education Department, Linda McMahon, has had her Senate confirmation hearing.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: After stinging election defeats, DNC eyes rural voters as key to 2026 midterm success
item.description: The new strategy, previewed exclusively to Fox News Digital, seeks to hold President Donald Trump accountable for policies that Democrats say are harmful to rural voters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senator calls RFK Jr.’s position on race and vaccines dangerous
item.description: In one of the most tense exchanges in a heated confirmation hearing, Senator Angela Alsobrooks called out past comments RFK Jr. made suggesting a different vaccine schedule for Black people.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone, MPR News

item.title: Pete Buttigieg blasts Trump after president excoriates him during press briefing
item.description: Former Transporation Sec. Pete Buttigieg blasted President Donald Trump after the commander in chief excoriated the Democrat during a press briefing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump health secretary nominee RFK Jr survives heated hearings ahead of crucial confirmation votes
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy, President Donald Trump's nominee for health and human services secretary, survives two heated confirmation hearings but still faces crucial Senate votes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US aid agency is in upheaval during foreign assistance freeze and staff departures
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Trump administration changes have upended the U.S. agency charged with providing humanitarian aid to countries overseas, with dozens of senior officials put on leave, thousands of contractors laid off, and a sweeping freeze imposed on billions of dollars in foreign assistance.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The DNC's outgoing chair says Democrats should have stuck with Joe Biden in 2024
item.description: The departing leader of the Democratic National Committee is reflecting on why his party lost to Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Meg Kinnard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Conspiracies, espionage, an enemies list: Takeaways from a wild day of confirmation hearings
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Conspiracy theories about vaccines. Secret meetings with dictators. An enemies list. President Donald Trump’ s most controversial Cabinet nominees — Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More migrants deported under Biden, Baja state official says
item.description: Tijuana Mayor Ismael Burgueño Ruiz says he will not lift an emergency declaration he made last month as a way to prepare for mass deportations from the United States.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's FBI chief pick, Kash Patel, insists he has no 'enemies list' and won't seek retribution
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Kash Patel, President Donald Trump’s pick to lead the FBI, insisted to deeply skeptical Democrats on Thursday that he did not have an “enemies list" and that the bureau under his leadership would not seek retribution against the president's adversaries or launch investigations for
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump VA pick Doug Collins advances to full Senate vote
item.description: Doug Collins, President Donald Trump's nominee for VA chief, passed a Senate cloture vote Thursday night and will proceed to a full vote on the Senate floor for confirmation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House aide says spending freeze isn't being rescinded despite memo
item.description: The White House is claiming the Wednesday move by the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) rescinding a controversial order that froze a wide swath of federal financial assistance is not actually an end to curbing government spending. “This is NOT a rescission of the federal funding freeze.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms Doug Burgum as interior secretary after Trump tasked him to boost drilling
item.description: The Senate confirmed Doug Burgum as interior secretary late Thursday after President Donald Trump tapped the North Dakota billionaire to spearhead the Republican administration's ambitions to boost fossil fuel production. The vote was 79-18.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Trump Blasts DEI at the FAA
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats press Army secretary nominee if ‘readiness’ affected by southern border deployments
item.description: Democrats sounded off about the White House sending U.S. troops to the southern border, but Army secretary nominee Daniel Driscoll insisted that he did not believe it would affect readiness.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP senator 'surprised' Gabbard refused to call Snowden a traitor
item.description: Sen. James Lankford (R-Okla.), a member of the Senate Intelligence Committee, said he was “surprised” that director of national intelligence nominee Tulsi Gabbard refused to call former NSA contractor Edward Snowden a “traitor” after being repeatedly pressed on the question at a Thursday hearing.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump’s briefing on National Airport plane crash
item.description: President Trump on Thursday addressed the deadly midair collision just outside Washington, D.C., as rescue crews searched for victims in the Potomac River and investigators worked to determine the cause of the crash.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Democrats revolt against Trump in fallout from foreign aid freeze
item.description: Senate Democrats are starting to revolt against President Trump’s agenda on Capitol Hill amid fallout from an initial freeze on all foreign assistance, warning Republicans that failure to work together will incur consequences. Sen. Chris Coons (D-Del.) on Thursday reversed support for Rep.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: President Trump casts blame during briefing on deadly plane crash
item.description: President Donald Trump started Thursday’s press briefing with a moment of silence for the victims and their families after a deadly crash involving an American Airlines plane and an Army helicopter near Ronald Reagan National Airport on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, Fox 5

item.title: Live updates: Trump takes aim at DEI after plane crash; Gabbard pressed on Snowden
item.description: President Trump on Thursday appeared to blame diversity initiatives at the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) for weakening safety following a deadly midair collision near Washington, D.C. Over two dozen bodies have been pulled from the water.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump suggests FAA's diversity efforts caused safety issues in crash
item.description: Trump weighed in on a crash between an American Airlines plane and a military helicopter, claiming it “should have been prevented.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump buyout plan faces legal, logistical roadblocks
item.description: The Trump administration’s efforts to shrink the federal government by offering what amounts to a buyout to federal employees is raising questions about its implementation and legality.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trans community fears Trump's actions will upend legal precedent on prison protections
item.description: One of President Trump's first executive orders impacts transgender inmates incarcerated in federal facilities. It's just one in a series of actions signed by Trump that target the trans community.
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz, MPR News

item.title: Despite Trump immigration policy changes, experts say the H-2A program is on solid footing
item.description: The Trump administration has started its overhaul of existing immigration policy. However, one program that allows migrant agricultural workers to work temporarily is for now, unaffected.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Trump's pulling a U-turn on EVs, but not much has changed
item.description: President Donald Trump has charted a new course for electric vehicle policy in the U.S. While the direction is clear, the details aren't.
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske, MPR News

item.title: Fewer than half in new poll trust Trump, RFK Jr. to make right health recommendations
item.description: The latest KFF survey finds 42 percent of respondents say they have either a great deal or fair amount of trust in Trump to make the right recommendations when it comes to health issues.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Trump orders Education, Labor and other departments to enhance school choice
item.description: The president directed several departments to submit plans for using federal funds to expand school choice.
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta, MPR News

item.title: Democrats demand Patel information in special counsel's classified documents report
item.description: Democrats on the Senate Judiciary Committee are demanding to see the volume of former special counsel Jack Smith’s report dealing with the Mar-a-Lago classified documents investigation, asserting it has relevant information about Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to lead the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s DOJ could shift key Supreme Court cases
item.description: President Trump’s Justice Department could change the department’s position on critical Supreme Court cases, potentially altering the trajectory of high-profile appeals before the justices.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez embraces role as attack dog against Trump
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez is embracing her role as one of the party's sharpest critics of President Trump, while other Democrats are working on a softer approach, with Ocasio-Cortez's actions showing her as the party's most aggressive member.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: Medicaid, SNAP, other benefits safe for Nevadans, Lombardo says
item.description: Gov. Joe Lombardo issued a statement Tuesday to clarify what is and is not affected by executive orders issued by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration to offer buyouts to all federal workers ahead of return to office
item.description: The Trump administration is offering all 2 million federal employees what amounts to a buyout if they do not intend to return to work in person later this year, sources confirmed to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NJ congressman launches plan to tackle rising meat and egg prices
item.description: FORT LEE, N.J. (PIX11) -- Food prices in the coming year are expected to continue to increase, according to the Department of Agriculture. It's unlikely to be the sharp rise we saw in the past few years, but certain products such as eggs and meats continue to rise more than most other groceries.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Florida Republicans end DeSantis special session before calling their own
item.description: Lawmakers in Tallahassee on Monday abruptly ended a special session called by Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis to address illegal immigration and other issues.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Measure to make California an independent country cleared to gather signatures
item.description: Another ballot measure asking whether California should be its own country is cleared to start gathering signatures, the California Secretary of State announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5
